codex_response:
Implemented the ARC2 package for `6bcdb01e` in [__init__.py](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_6bcdb01e/__init__.py#L1), [verifier.py](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_6bcdb01e/verifier.py#L1), and [generator.py](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_6bcdb01e/generator.py#L1). The rule is: extend the border `3` seed inward in a straight line, and whenever the next cell is an `8`, treat a diagonal `8` pair as a `/` or `\` mirror and turn the ray accordingly. I used the `arc2_opus46_summary.json` hint because it matched the official examples; `arc2_sonnet45_summary.jsonl` had no entry, so no `notes.md` was needed. No new shared primitive was added.

The generator stays on-family by using square `5-9` grids, a length-2 border seed, multi-turn mirror paths, color-fixed `7/8/3` layouts, and a small amount of unused `8` noise. Verification passed on `3/3` official examples, the shared build produced `1000` generated examples, `./arc2_verify_generated_all.sh --task 6bcdb01e` passed `1000/1000`, and I visually checked [preview_01.png](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/6bcdb01e/previews/preview_01.png) and it looks on-family.
